decision: Resolve A-vs-B as a HARD-SEAM HYBRID, not a blend. The "spin" CLI is a real TypeScript/Node executable (npx-installable, esbuild-bundled, vitest-tested, commander-based — mirroring OpenSpec's exact ship mechanics) that is the deterministic state machine and gatekeeper, and it NEVER calls a model. The plugin's slash commands are the only model-execution layer; they call spin for every ordering, validation, gate, and state decision and branch strictly on exit code, and they fan out worker subagents via the Task tool. Pure-B (better markdown) is rejected because "tested end-to-end" is impossible against prose: you cannot write a vitest assertion that an LLM ordered files by dependency or honored a checkbox — and AgentSpec's current build.md proves the failure mode (topo-ordering as "Step 3" reasoning, "max 3" retry as prose line 199, a self-marked checkbox gate at lines 206-216). Pure-A (CLI runs the agents) is rejected harder because end users of a Claude Code plugin have no SDK key, no dispatch endpoint, and no Workflow runtime; any node code that "runs the agent" is either the fake api.anthropic.com/dispatch fantasy from ECC's autonomous-agent-harness or a non-deterministic `claude -p` shell-out that breaks testability and the plugin sandbox. The seam is the whole design: deterministic concerns (Kahn topo-order over schema.yaml, structural validation, named gates, run-state) live in spin with an exit-code ABI (0 pass / 1 gate-blocked / 2 usage / 3 internal); authoring concerns (specs, code, analysis) live in commands via Task; and schemas/ is the shared editable workflow definition consumed by both. Every arrow in the command protocol (spin next -> Task fan-out -> spin complete --handoff -> spin gate) is a CLI call a test can assert.
rationale: All three proposals converge on the hybrid and all three correctly reject the ECC fake-dispatch anti-pattern, so the decision is not in doubt — the differentiation is in the keystones that make it actually testable and crash-safe. Proposal 1 (Opus) is the strongest: it alone identifies the two load-bearing mechanisms the baseline left soft — (a) a CLI-WRITTEN run-state ledger (spin.run.json) so gates read filesystem+state, never conversation memory, making them idempotent and re-runnable after a mid-session plugin crash (this is OpenSpec's detectCompleted file-existence insight extended to gates), and (b) `spin complete <id> --handoff out.json` as the single point where worker output is validated against a Zod handoff schema BEFORE the artifact counts done. Proposals 2 and 3 both name the handoff JSON contract but neither makes the CLI the enforcer at a specific command boundary, and neither makes run-state crash-safe — both lean on `spin state complete` as an LLM-invoked mutation with no validation gate in between, which reopens the exact "LLM self-marks done" hole they set out to close. Proposal 3 contributes the decisive fidelity constraint the other two underweight: AgentSpec's real value is 58 agents + 24 KB domains + deep DE surface (verified by Bash: 58/24/31), and the harness must preserve that roster intact while hardening control flow — a thinner harness that loses the domain depth is a net loss. Proposal 3 also correctly separates the human-facing markdown artifact (DEFINE.md) from the machine-facing handoff sidecar (.spindle/handoffs/define.json) the gate evaluates, which removes the brittleness of regex-scraping markdown for a clarity score. The locked design therefore takes Proposal 1's run-state+gate-ABI+complete--handoff keystone, Proposal 3's preserve-the-roster + sidecar-handoff discipline, and Proposal 2's progressive-adoption ergonomics (commands detect .spindle/ and degrade gracefully), while porting OpenSpec's graph.ts/schema.ts/types.ts/state.ts verbatim (confirmed clean, MIT, Zod-validated, cycle-detecting) and adding parallel_group + handoff + gates fields the SDD workflow needs.

PORTED: artifactOutputExists (path/glob existence check) supporting detectCompleted.
NEW KEYSTONE: atomic read/write of .spindle/run.json (current schema, feature, completed set, retry counters, gate ledger). CLI-written only, never LLM-written.
NEW: Zod RunState schema validating .spindle/run.json on every read.
NEW: gate id -> GateFn(ctx:RunContext)=>GateResult map. RunContext = {runDir, runState, graph, readFile} — filesystem+state only.
NEW: runGate(id)->GateResult; maps passed=false to exit 1 with JSON {gate,passed,reasons,unmet}.

OPUS-CRITICAL: G_DEFINE/G_DESIGN/G_BUILD/G_SHIP. G_BUILD does real file-existence over manifest + criteria set-diff, replacing build.md prose checkbox. G_SHIP is define.criteria minus build.passed must be empty.
NEW: G_KB_STRUCTURE (manifest/index/quick-reference/>=1 concept exist), G_KB_COVERAGE (every manifest concept present as file + >=N test-cases). Replaces create-kb blind delegation.
OPUS-CRITICAL: G_ROUTER_COVERAGE asserts bijection — every agent frontmatter parsed (Zod), each appears exactly once in routing output, no silent skips (fixes Python router [WARN] Skipping path).
OPUS-CRITICAL: G_REVIEW_BLOCK counts surviving CRITICAL findings after adversarial pass over validated Finding[]; shared by /review and /migrate. Security-critical.
NEW: assert required ## sections exist and are non-empty in a markdown artifact.
NEW: parse+Zod-validate agent/skill frontmatter; replaces fragile regex parse in generate-agent-router.py; fail-closed, no silent skip.
OPUS-CRITICAL: pure set-diff of DEFINE acceptance-criteria IDs vs BUILD results with status=passed; returns unmet[]. Backbone of G_SHIP.
NEW: Zod for every worker output contract (Define/Design/BuildTask/Finding/Claim/MigrationPlan/AdversaryReport/KBConcept/ClaudeMdSection/AgentFrontmatter).
NEW: validate a worker-output JSON file against a named handoff schema; used by spin complete --handoff and spin handoff-check.
NEW: pure resolver taskKind+budget -> {tier,model,reason}. Critical kinds (adversary/architect/review-judge) never resolve below their floor; downgrades allowed only where a gate backstops output.
NEW: Zod types for routing policy + override block.
public exports for tests and reuse.

schemaFormat: Ported verbatim from OpenSpec's schemas/*/schema.yaml + templates/*.md pattern (confirmed: name, version, description, artifacts[] with id/generates/description/template/instruction?/requires[], plus optional apply phase; loaded+Zod-validated by schema.ts with duplicate-id, dangling-requires, and DFS cycle checks). Extended with FOUR fields the SDD workflow needs that OpenSpec lacks: (1) model: per-artifact authoring tier hint (opus/sonnet/haiku) consumed by the command and surfaced by `spin next`; (2) handoff: path to schemas/handoffs/<id>.json — the typed worker-output contract that `spin complete --handoff` enforces in code BEFORE the artifact counts done; (3) validate: declarative structural checks (md_sections list, criteria_ids prefix, manifest_table bool) run by `spin validate`; (4) parallel_group: marks an artifact whose workers fan out as a wave (e.g. build by manifest layer, KB concepts) — this finally EXECUTES OpenSpec's getNextArtifacts wave instead of only displaying it. Plus a top-level config block (e.g. build_retry_cap:3 — caps as DATA not prose) and a gates map (before_design:G_DEFINE, before_build:G_DESIGN, before_ship:[G_BUILD,G_SHIP]). Same engine drives schemas/sdd and schemas/kb, so KB creation is just another artifact graph. Editing a workflow = editing one YAML + its templates; `spin schema validate` Zod-checks it. Example sdd artifact: {id:build, generates:.spindle/features/{feature}/BUILD_REPORT.md, template:build-report.md, model:sonnet, parallel_group:by_manifest_layer, handoff:handoffs/build-task.handoff.json, requires:[design]}.

cliCommands.purpose: Scaffold .spindle/ run dir, copy editable schema, create run.json.
Print .spindle/run.json (current schema, feature, completed set, retry counters, gate ledger).
Full Kahn build order for inspection/debug.
KEYSTONE: validate worker handoff JSON against the artifact's schema FIRST, then mark complete in run.json. exit 1 if handoff invalid.
Structural validation (md sections / frontmatter / manifest table). exit 0/1 + JSON issues.
Run named gate against filesystem+run-state. exit 0 pass / 1 BLOCK with {gate,passed,reasons,unmet}.
Set-diff DEFINE acceptance criteria vs BUILD passed results -> unmet[].
Increment / check retry counter in run-state against config cap (powers bounded-loop). --ok exit 1 when ceiling hit.
Inspect / Zod-validate the editable workflow schema.

gates.blocksIf: DEFINE missing required ## sections (Why/What/Acceptance Criteria) OR zero acceptance criteria with stable AC-n IDs OR define handoff fails Zod
DESIGN missing file-manifest table OR a manifest row lacks file/action/purpose OR no Decisions section
any manifest file not present on disk OR criteria-diff(DEFINE,BUILD) non-empty OR BUILD_REPORT missing — replaces build.md prose checkbox + max-3 retry
define.criteria minus build.passed is non-empty (unmet acceptance criteria) OR SHIPPED artifact incomplete
worker output JSON fails its declared handoff Zod schema
domain dir missing manifest/index/quick-reference or has zero concept files
a manifest-declared concept has no file OR test-cases below configured N
any agent frontmatter invalid OR an agent missing from routing OR an agent appears more than once (bijection broken) — no silent skip
count of surviving CRITICAL findings over validated Finding[] is greater than 0

modelRouting: Routing is first-class, pure (src/core/model-route/policy.ts), unit-tested, consumed two ways: static model: per artifact/agent, and dynamic `spin route <task-kind>`. Default to the cheapest tier that can VERIFIABLY do the task. TABLE — HAIKU (mechanical, gate-backstopped): file read, structure extraction, frontmatter parse, template-fill, format conversion, claim extraction, ship/archive prose, sync-context section scans, router string assembly. SONNET (analysis/authoring/synthesis): spec authoring, design synthesis (impl sections), code build per file, KB concept authoring, finding analysis, claim verification, migration-plan authoring, deterministic merges. OPUS (deepest reasoning + adversarial): architecture decisions (DEFINE/DESIGN intent + ADRs), the _adversary/challenger role, migration equivalence-break, council/critic, review-judge on CRITICAL. TWO ENFORCED PRINCIPLES: (a) the verifier outranks-or-equals the generator on any critical gate — an Opus adversary challenges a Sonnet build; the cheaper tier is never the final judge of a CRITICAL finding; (b) tier downgrades are free ONLY when a deterministic gate backstops the output — Haiku does extraction because spin validate catches its mistakes; --budget low shifts authoring Sonnet->Haiku only where a gate exists and NEVER downgrades the adversary or architect kinds. policy.test.ts asserts each task-kind->tier and that critical kinds never resolve below their floor.

coverage.harnessApproach: schemas/sdd brainstorm artifact (optional, model:opus); spin next yields it; brainstorm-worker authors; spin complete; no blocking gate.
define-worker (opus) writes DEFINE.md + define handoff sidecar; spin complete --handoff validates; spin gate G_DEFINE (sections + AC-n IDs) blocks /design.
spin gate G_DEFINE first; design-worker (opus) writes manifest; spin complete --handoff; spin gate G_DESIGN (manifest table) blocks /build.

spin next + parallel_group fan-out of build-workers (sonnet) by manifest layer via Task; bounded-loop capped by config.build_retry_cap via spin retry counters (run-state, not prose); spin gate G_BUILD (file-existence + criteria diff) replaces checkbox; --judge passthrough to judge.py preserved.
spin diff-criteria + spin gate G_SHIP block on unmet criteria in code; haiku ship-worker writes archive prose; run-state marks all complete.

schemas/kb artifact graph manifest->concepts(parallel wave)->patterns->test-cases->index; concept-workers (sonnet) parallel; G_KB_STRUCTURE + G_KB_COVERAGE replace create-kb blind delegation to kb-architect.
parallel arch+security+CodeRabbit workers -> Finding[] (finding.handoff); spin dedups by file+line in code; opus challenger on CRITICAL; G_REVIEW_BLOCK gates.
parallel dbt+spark plan workers in independent contexts -> MigrationPlan; independent equivalence-worker (not the author); opus adversary tries to break chosen plan; G_REVIEW_BLOCK on CRITICAL=0.
haiku extract-worker -> Claim[] (claim.handoff); parallel verify-workers per claim; parallel correction pass; spin only sequences+validates.
5 parallel section-workers (haiku) -> ClaudeMdSection handoffs; deterministic merge honoring preserve/replace/merge rules in code, not LLM free-merge.

spin parses every agent frontmatter with Zod (frontmatter.ts) replacing fragile regex Python; G_ROUTER_COVERAGE asserts bijection (every agent exactly once, none invalid, no silent skip) before routing.json is written.
criteria-diff.ts set-diff in TypeScript via G_SHIP — eliminates LLM passing an ambiguous checklist.

coverage.capability: 58-agent roster + 24 KB domains + DE command surface
coverage.harnessApproach: ported intact (plugin/agents, plugin/kb, plugin/commands/data-engineering) with added output_schema frontmatter; harness hardens control flow without diluting domain depth (Proposal 3 fidelity constraint).

buildPlan.detail: Create repo, package.json (bin spin, esbuild build.js, vitest), tsconfig, bin/spin.js, LICENSE(MIT), CI yml. Mirror OpenSpec exactly so npx/node install works offline.

buildPlan.phase: P1 Port the deterministic spine VERBATIM
buildPlan.detail: Copy OpenSpec graph.ts/schema.ts/types.ts/state.ts/outputs.ts into src/core/artifact-graph; port their unit tests. Run vitest green before changing anything. This is the proven Kahn+Zod+cycle core.

Add model/handoff/validate/parallel_group/config/gates fields to types.ts Zod; build run-state.ts (atomic, schema-validated, retry counters) + run-state.test.ts. This is the crash-safe state keystone.
md-section-validator, frontmatter (Zod, fail-closed), criteria-diff, handoff/schemas.ts + handoff-check, with full unit tests. These back every gate.
registry + gate-runner; author sdd-gates (opus — security-adjacent correctness), kb-gates, router-gate (opus), review-gate (opus). Each gate gets pass+fail+idempotent-rerun tests.
policy.ts pure resolver + policy.test.ts asserting floors and gate-backed downgrades.
commander index.ts + command modules mapping Result->exit-code ABI; cli.test.ts spawns subprocess and asserts exit codes + stdout JSON.
Author schemas/sdd/schema.yaml (encodes WORKFLOW_CONTRACTS.yaml as data) + schemas/kb/schema.yaml + handoff JSON Schemas; port templates from AgentSpec sdd/templates and kb/_templates.
Copy 58 agents (add output_schema frontmatter), 24 KB domains, DE/core/visual commands that stay intact, skills, judge.py, hooks. Preserves domain depth.

build.md (parallel_group + bounded-loop + G_BUILD), ship.md (G_SHIP), create-kb.md (kb graph), review.md/migrate.md (adversarial + G_REVIEW_BLOCK), fact-check.md, sync-context.md, gen-router.md. Author worker agents + _adversary/challenger.
harness-protocol, model-routing, adversarial-gate, bounded-loop, parallel-fanout — ported from ECC good skills, explicitly NOT the fake-dispatch one.

sdd-cycle.e2e.test.ts + kb-cycle.e2e.test.ts: drive full cycle through the CLI with fixtures incl. a deliberately-broken manifest; assert G_BUILD exit 1 then exit 0; final run-state complete. Add grep-guard test failing the build if src/ contains fetch/anthropic/claude -p.
node build.js + vitest run green, coverage 80%+ on src/core, npx-install smoke test of the bundled CLI offline.

e2eTestStrategy: The deliverable test is test/e2e/sdd-cycle.e2e.test.ts: it drives the FULL 5-phase cycle through the real CLI (spawning node bin/spin.js subprocesses), simulating the model layer with deterministic fixture files the test itself writes — including one deliberately-broken state (only 1 of 2 manifest files present). Sequence: spin init --schema sdd; spin next -> assert ready includes define; write DEFINE fixture + define handoff; spin validate define exit 0; spin complete define --handoff (asserts G_HANDOFF); spin gate G_DEFINE exit 0; spin next -> ready:[design]; write DESIGN with manifest; spin complete design --handoff; spin gate G_DESIGN exit 0; spin next -> ready:[build]; write ONLY file1 of the manifest; spin gate G_BUILD -> ASSERT EXIT 1 with unmet:[file2] (proves it BLOCKS); write file2 + BUILD_REPORT; spin gate G_BUILD exit 0; spin diff-criteria -> unmet:[]; spin gate G_SHIP exit 0; spin state -> phase:ship, complete:all. This asserts the harness blocks when it should and only advances when state is real — the property no prose-only design can be tested for. Parallel kb-cycle.e2e.test.ts (G_KB_COVERAGE blocks until every manifest concept exists) and router-gen coverage (inject duplicate agent -> G_ROUTER_COVERAGE exit 1). Plus: cli.test.ts asserts the exit-code ABI per command; every gate has a pass+fail+idempotent-rerun unit test (run twice from identical files -> identical result, proving crash-safety since gates read filesystem+run-state, never conversation). A grep-guard test fails CI if src/ contains fetch(/anthropic/claude -p, enforcing the no-model-calls invariant. CI runs node build.js + vitest run on every push with 80% coverage gate on src/core. No LLM, network, or mock-API needed — runs in seconds.
adversarialFindings: PROPOSAL 1 (Opus) worst flaw: it under-commits on PRESERVING the 58-agent/24-KB domain depth — its file tree lists only ~6 worker agents and folds the rest into terse category dirs, risking a harness that is mechanically superior but strips the exact specialist content (dbt/spark/lakehouse/cloud agents, 24 KB domains) that is AgentSpec's whole reason to exist. RESOLVED: locked spec ports the full roster intact (plugin/agents _roster_de/PORT.md + plugin/kb/PORT.md) with only an added output_schema frontmatter field, adopting Proposal 3's fidelity constraint while keeping Proposal 1's spine.

PROPOSAL 2 (ergonomics) worst flaw: its gates and state are LLM-invoked mutations with NO validation between worker output and `spin state complete <id>` — `spin validate <file> --schema` and `spin state complete` are separate optional steps a command can skip, so a worker can produce garbage and the command can still mark the artifact done, reopening the self-marking hole it set out to close. It also reintroduces a regex-scraped clarity score (G_DEFINE_CLARITY reads a score line from markdown) which is exactly the brittle prose-coupling to avoid. RESOLVED: locked spec makes `spin complete --handoff` a SINGLE atomic command that validates the handoff Zod schema BEFORE marking complete (no skippable seam), and evaluates structure from a machine-facing handoff sidecar JSON, not a scraped markdown score.

PROPOSAL 3 (fidelity) worst flaw: it relies on a separate human-markdown artifact PLUS a sidecar handoff JSON but never makes the run-state CRASH-SAFE or the gates IDEMPOTENT — gates read .spindle/state.json and retry counters, but nothing guarantees those survive a mid-session plugin death or that re-running a gate from the same files yields the same verdict; its e2e test marks artifacts done via `spin mark-done` with no handoff validation in the marking step. RESOLVED: locked spec adopts Proposal 1's rule that gates read filesystem+run-state ONLY (never conversation), adds idempotent-re-run unit tests for every gate, and makes run-state.ts atomic + Zod-validated on every read.
residualRisks: INSTALL REACH (highest): a Claude Code plugin ships markdown and does not auto-run npm install, so spin must reach the user's shell. Locked default: commit prebuilt dist/ in the plugin and invoke via node "${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/dist/cli/index.js" (offline, self-contained, matches how AgentSpec already ships scripts/judge.py); a SessionStart hook runs node build.js if dist/ is missing. npx @agentspec/spin is the documented fallback. Must be validated by an offline smoke test in P12.

WORKER NON-COMPLIANCE: a subagent may return prose instead of the JSON handoff. Mitigated because spin complete --handoff exits 1 on invalid JSON, forcing the command into bounded-loop re-dispatch — but the command body must actually branch on that exit code; a sloppily-authored command could ignore it. Mitigation: harness-protocol skill makes exit-code branching mandatory and gen-router/ci could lint commands for the pattern (future).

SIDECAR DRIFT: the human-facing markdown artifact and the machine handoff JSON can diverge (worker writes a good DEFINE.md but a stale handoff). Gates trust the JSON; humans read the markdown. Mitigation: validate command can cross-check key fields (criteria IDs) appear in both; not fully closed in MVP.

SCHEMA-FORK FOOTGUN: users editing schemas/sdd/schema.yaml can introduce a cycle or dangling require; spin schema validate catches it (DFS cycle + dangling-ref checks ported), but only if the command/hook runs it before use. Mitigation: spin next/gate call parseSchema which throws SchemaValidationError on load.

COST: Opus on define/design/build-adversary is the default; on large features the adversarial + opus-authoring passes are the dominant spend. --budget low downgrades gated authoring to Haiku/Sonnet but never the adversary; users on a tight budget must opt in explicitly.
